page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, ensuring consistent execution without hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory throughput. The instance achieves top-tier results in uncached memory bandwidth, reaching 402.56 GB/sec for read operations and 198.42 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-threaded operations perform exceptionally well, as evidenced by top-tier scores in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression. Single-core performance remains in the average tier, though cryptographic operations via AES instructions are supported. Network performance is a key highlight, featuring a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and high memory bandwidth relative to its power profile. This makes the instance highly efficient for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads. It is particularly suited for high-throughput static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded data compression tasks. The 150 Gbps network baseline also makes it ideal for distributed systems and network-optimized virtual appliances.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory read/write bandwidth, and strong database operations. It is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high network throughput and parallel processing. This instance is well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded data compression.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB of RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 48 dedicated cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, compute-optimized ARM64 platform designed for demanding multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput network applications.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores
Equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
